TextENGNew: When he first heard the story, he was genuinely torn. ...It was true that he hated the thought of losing his pension.
But... in the end, Ooishi made his decision. What tipped the scales was the news Yamaoki had delivered yesterday, about Tomoe...
``......Huh?@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1037. Y-you mean... that whole arrest operation at Kakiuchi Airport... Tomoe-chan actually avenged her parents?``
``That's right.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bc_yamaoki1002. Spanning twelve long years... that girl finally pulled it off.``
Ooishi had already heard about the Kakiuchi Airport incident through television and other media.
Moreover, there were clear indications that major corporations or powerful individuals lurked behind the dealings, which explained why such heavy media restrictions had been imposed.
But he never could have imagined that a conspiracy and scheme of that magnitude had been unfolding behind the scenes.
``It was the detective's soul and iron will she inherited from her father that turned the impossible into reality.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bc_yamaoki1004. ...I couldn't be more proud of her.``
``She'll come to give you a proper report when the time is right, but I wanted you to be the first to know...``
For twelve years after losing her parents... Tomoe had held fast to her convictions and never given up.
Of course, one could argue that an aging man like himself and a young woman like her stood to lose very different things in exchange for such dedication.
But... just like Yamaoki, Ooishi had his own pride.
The pride of a detective who had taught Tomoe the meaning of justice and striven to serve as a model worthy of emulation.
If that was the case... then he could not betray those very principles himself.
After speaking so grandly about justice, how could he face Tomoe if he lived in a way that contradicted everything he had said...?
That fierce, burning resolve... stirred Ooishi's faltering heart and set it ablaze once more.
And so, at dawn, Ooishi summoned the old forensics specialist and Kumagai to a separate room and laid everything bare.

``I'll say it again -- this is a very dangerous bridge to cross.@k@vS14/11/220700116. ...If I get off with a reprimand, that's one thing, but a formal disciplinary action would cost me my entire pension.``

``Ha ha ha ha!@k@vS14/00/223000002. Oh, come on, Ooishi.@k@vS14/00/223000003. I hear that before every dangerous operation, you drag your subordinates to the mahjong parlor next door and give them the same warning.@k@vS14/00/223000004. This is no different. Right?``

``Yeah, that's right, Ooishi-san!@k@vS14/19/VTD_kumagai_0002. Why don't you do it with your usual gusto -- 'Anyone who values their pension has ten seconds to walk out'!@k@vS14/19/VTD_kumagai_0003. It doesn't feel right otherwise!``
``......No, this really is dangerous, Kuma-chan.@k@vS14/11/220700118. Are you truly prepared for this...?!``

``Then try counting to ten and see what happens!@k@vS14/19/VTD_kumagai_0005. Fine, I'll count this time!!@k@vS14/19/VTD_kumagai_0006. Anyone who values their pension, leave before I count to ten!@k@vS14/19/VTD_kumagai_0007. One~, two~, three~!!``
``O-okay, okay, I get it!@k@vS14/11/220700120. I understand your resolve, Kuma-chan!@k@vS14/11/220700121. But really, are you sure about this?``
``Absolutely! Ever since I heard Akasaka-san's war stories that day, I've been fired up!``
``I became a detective to fight against evil!@k@vS14/19/223100010. And someday, I want to be a man with heroic tales of my own to tell, just like Akasaka-san!!``

``A dangerous mission? Bring it on!@k@vS14/19/223100012. Besides, every operation you've ever planned has succeeded, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/19/223100013. So if you think this one's worth doing, then I'm not scared one bit!``

``Ha ha ha ha!@k@vS14/00/223000006. It's wonderful to be young!@k@vS14/00/223000007. This fellow here's been quite inspired by Akasaka-kun ever since that day.@k@vS14/00/223000008. He keeps going on about wanting to be part of secret investigations and covert cases.``
``...Ha... hahhh...@k@vS14/11/220700123. Well, nahahahaha, youth is a fine thing indeed...@k@vS14/11/220700124. ...And what about you, old-timer?``

``Ha! Count me in!@k@vS14/00/223000010. From what you've told me, the victim's autopsy could make or break whether we can drag those scoundrels in.@k@vS14/00/223000011. I've been living recklessly up to now anyway.``
``At my age, this old bag of bones won't last much longer anyway.@k@vS14/00/223000013. So if I was born a man, I might as well go out with a bang!``
``...Good grief...@k@vS14/11/220700126. You two really are fearless...@k@vS14/11/220700127. That's why you declare riichi even with three dora tiles showing...``

``What are you talking about, Ooishi-san?!@k@vS14/19/223100015. 'A real man knows when he can't back down. If you're going to discard, discard right down the middle!'@k@vS14/19/223100016. That's your own catchphrase!``
``Mm-hmm. Can't argue with that!``
``All right.@k@vS14/11/220700129. ...Your necks are in my hands now. I'll take full responsibility!``

``...Now that that's settled, I'll need to make some preparations of my own.@k@vS14/00/223000016. With our current staff and equipment, analyzing drugs is beyond our reach.@k@vS14/00/223000017. I'll put in some calls to forensics contacts and round up whoever's available.``
``...Ooishi, once this is all over, you're treating me to something big, you hear?``

``Absolutely!!@k@vS14/11/220700131. You can count on that.@k@vS14/11/220700132. But please be very careful about who you confide in, all right...?@k@vS14/11/220700133. Apparently there are spies planted within the station.``
``I know. These old eyes aren't so easily fooled!@k@vS14/00/223000020. Leave it to me!``
``All riiiight, I'm fired up!!@k@vS14/19/223100018. A detective's soul, burning at full blaze!!``
...It had taken Ooishi an entire night to reach his decision, yet his senior and junior colleague had both agreed on the spot. He couldn't help wondering if that made him the indecisive one...
``Well then, I have some errands to attend to, so I'll be heading out.@k@vS14/11/220700135. Sorry for springing such an outrageous request on you.``

``Think nothing of it!@k@vS14/00/223000022. It's been ages since my blood got pumping like this.@k@vS14/00/223000023. In all my years, this is the first grand stage I've had to really test my mettle.@k@vS14/00/223000024. Far from being scared, I intend to enjoy every moment of it!``
``Same here! Let's do this, Ooishi-san!!``
``...Thank you, old-timer. Kuma-chan.``
Ooishi bowed deeply in gratitude to the two of them, who responded with resolute smiles...
``...I see.@k@vS14/03/220300044. All right, I think I have a good grasp of the situation.``
After listening to my and Rika-chan's full explanation, Mion slowly nodded. Then she walked up to me with purpose, faced me squarely, and smiled.
``Oh, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/220300046. About that --``
``What is it?``
*whack*
She suddenly pointed behind me, and when I turned to look, a light impact struck the back of my head. I whipped back around to find Mion glaring at me with her fist still cocked from the blow.

``Listen, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/220300048. I know you kept this secret because you were thinking of us. I told you that before.@k@vS14/03/220300049. ...But does that mean we're really so unreliable to you?``
``Th-that's not it at all!@k@vS14/01/220100111. It's just...``
``Just... what?@k@vS14/03/220300051. Let me be clear -- when you speak to me seriously, I will listen to the very end. I would never make light of it.``
``And if you ever ask for my help, I'm prepared to give it everything I have.@k@vS14/03/220300053. That's what I've always tried to do.``
``But if you ever doubted me, even for a moment, then I'll step down as your ally right here and now.@k@vS14/03/220300055. So which is it?!``
Mion's expression grew harsher as she spoke, her anger building visibly. Seeing her like that, I couldn't help but feel a pang of guilt.
``...I'm sorry, Mion.@k@vS14/01/220100113. And everyone. Whatever our reasons, it's true that we were overly cautious and consulted you too late.@k@vS14/01/220100114. ...I'm sorry.``
``Hau~, that's not the point.@k@vS14/02/220200029. Mii-chan is upset because you didn't trust her as much as you trusted Rika-chan. Isn't that right~?``
``N-no, that's not...!@k@vS14/03/220300057. I mean, well... maybe a little... yeah...``
Mion fidgeted and pouted her lips. Seeing that, Rena squealed 'so kyuuute~!' to tease her, then immediately turned serious again.
``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS14/02/220200031. You once told me that true friends share their burdens with each other.@k@vS14/02/220200032. Rena feels exactly the same way.``
``Having Keiichi-kun and Rika-chan lean on me isn't a burden at all. In fact, it makes me happy when you ask for my help.@k@vS14/02/220200034. Because that's proof you trust Rena.``

``So please, don't hold back before you've even said anything just because you're worried about causing trouble.@k@vS14/02/220200036. Everyone has secrets they don't want to share. But it can hurt just as much to realize someone is holding back from you, you know?``
``Sorry... you're right.@k@vS14/01/220100116. I can't practice what I preach, can I...``
As I said that and bowed my head meekly, Rika-chan stepped forward as if to shield me.

``Mii, and Rena.@k@vS14/05/220500188. ...I was the one who asked Keiichi to keep this from you.@k@vS14/05/220500189. Until we understood the enemy's movements and intentions, I thought it was better not to stir up unnecessary hostility.``
``If word got out and made the enemy suspicious, it could have put us in even greater danger.``
``I understand that.@k@vS14/03/220300059. ...But this is a matter of the heart, not the head.``
``...Well, I'll make sure you pay me back for this later.@k@vS14/03/220300061. Brace yourself, Kei-chan.``
``That's right.@k@vS14/02/220200038. Understood, Keiichi-kun?``
Mion and Rena gave an awkward shrug, then softened their expressions into warm smiles. ...Yeah, I really am glad to have these two as my friends.
``...However, regarding Nii-nii's situation, I believe that was clearly a result of Rika's inadequate explanation.``
``Had things been handled a bit more carefully, Shion-san wouldn't have lost control like that, and the Coach surely could have avoided getting hurt, don't you think?``
``Mii... I am truly sorry about that.``
Rika-chan looked dejected as she bowed her head once more to apologize to the Coach, who sat receiving treatment in the back of the room, and to Shion, who was bandaging him.
Fortunately, the gunshot wounds were shallow, and the Coach had already performed basic first aid and stopped the bleeding himself.

``Please, don't apologize.@k@vS14/10/22100256. ...If anything, all of this started because of my own failings.@k@vS14/10/22100257. No matter how many times I apologize to Shion-san and Satoko-chan, it will never be enough.``
``That's quite all right, Coach.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1017. ...Just knowing that Nii-nii is alive is enough to make me truly happy.``
``Right, Shion-san...?``

But Shion kept her head bowed in silence as she continued winding the bandage around the Coach's shoulder. Her expression was dark, and Satoko, who had tried to brighten the mood, clouded over with worry when she noticed.
``...Listen, Shion.@k@vS14/03/220300063. I truly understand how you feel, but even Satoko is saying this.@k@vS14/03/220300064. Right now, we need to discuss what comes next, okay?``
``Shii-chan......``
``...I know that perfectly well.@k@vS14/06/220600153. But there are things I just can't accept. Even when my head understands, my heart can't keep up...``
``Shii... please.@k@vS14/05/220500193. Please, lend us your strength.``
``The rest of you might be fine with this, but I'm not that mature.@k@vS14/06/220600155. After everything... after all that effort...``
``...That's enough, Shion!@k@vS14/03/220300066. Could you at least try to consider how the Coach must feel?``
``Sh-Shion-san......!``
Satoko's eyes welled with pleading tears. Seeing that, Shion abruptly raised her head, turned to face Satoko, and spoke.
``That's what I'm saying -- I can't accept it!``
``Hey, Shion--!``
``Why did Satoko go back to calling me 'Shion-san'?!@k@vS14/06/220600158. She was just calling me 'Nee-nee' a little while ago, wasn't she?!``
Everyone fell speechless, momentarily unable to comprehend what Shion had just said.
``Uh, um... Shion... san?``
``See, there it is again!!@k@vS14/06/220600160. Satoko, are you toying with me?@k@vS14/06/220600161. You call me 'Nee-nee' and get me all excited, only to coldly push me away right after!``
``Is that your game? Lift me up with happiness, then reel me in with cold indifference?@k@vS14/06/220600163. Well, congratulations -- it worked perfectly on me!``
``But even so, don't you think you could show at least a scrap of tenderness at a time like this? Am I wrong? Well?``
``Or what, are you stockpiling this as leverage to get out of your carrot-and-asparagus lunches in the future?!@k@vS14/06/220600166. Hmm?!``
``Sh-Shii-chan...@k@vS14/02/220200042. Sorry, but... is this the part where we're supposed to laugh? Is it?``
``......Should I be angry instead?@k@vS14/03/220300069. Or should I just smack you?@k@vS14/03/220300070. You utterly clueless girl...!!``
``Look who's talking.``
I couldn't help tossing that retort out, and Mion's backfist connected squarely.
``Huh? Wh-what is it, Rika?``
``Could you just call her that for now, please?@k@vS14/05/220500197. This conversation is going absolutely nowhere...``
Rika-chan pressed her fingers to her temple in exasperation and gave Satoko a light tap on the shoulder. Well, this was still far better than an actual bloodbath...

...And so, for a while, Satoko -- face beet-red -- obliged Shion by calling her 'Nee-nee' to her heart's content. Shion's earlier foul mood vanished entirely, replaced by a blissfully beaming smile and a purring 'Nyaaan~'...
``...Anyway, let's get back on track.@k@vS14/01/220100123. Is there anyone here who still needs further explanation?``
I said that and looked around at everyone's faces. They all wore the same wide-eyed, stunned expressions.
...Then again, I couldn't blame them. The story was so outlandish that it would have been perfectly natural if I had followed it up with 'Just kidding~!'
Of course... if I actually said that, even as a joke, every last one of them would beat me senseless.

``To be perfectly honest, as acting head of the Sonozaki family, there's quite a lot I'd like to ask.@k@vS14/03/220300072. ...About the motives behind these incidents, the secrets lurking beneath the surface.@k@vS14/03/220300073. But for now, I'll set all of that aside.``

``In any case, our top priority is securing Rika-chan's safety.@k@vS14/03/220300075. Followed by intercepting the enemy.@k@vS14/03/220300076. Let's focus on these two objectives and plan our next moves.``

Mion swept her gaze around at Rena and the others, then turned back to me and nodded. For now, she believed that Rika-chan and I were sincere. I could read it in her eyes, and that alone gave me immeasurable reassurance.

``That said, taking shelter at my place was a smart call.@k@vS14/03/220300078. Our basement could withstand a nuclear war.@k@vS14/03/220300079. It has electricity, a phone line.@k@vS14/03/220300080. And of course, plenty of food!``
``If these Mountain Dogs are as formidable as you say, then there's no safer place in this village to hide Rika-chan than our house.``
``......I'm sorry for dragging all of you into this...``
Rika-chan said it as if apologizing from the depths of her soul.
...Up until now, Rika-chan had kept the existence of this bizarre disease called Hinamizawa Syndrome, and her connection to the shadowy organization called 'Tokyo,' completely hidden from us.
She had resolved to shoulder that burden alone rather than get any of us involved.
Even when she finally confided in me, she had been reluctant -- and that was only natural, since she had never once managed to prevail against this enemy.
Even so, I rallied my spirits and looked around at everyone.
``Rika-chan, enough of that already!``

``Honestly, the scale of this thing did throw me for a loop at first.@k@vS14/01/220100126. ...But hey, everyone?@k@vS14/01/220100127. Deep down, you're all thinking this is getting pretty exciting, aren't you?``

``Satoko, it sounds like they're giving us permission to go all out.@k@vS14/01/220100129. You've been holding back, haven't you?@k@vS14/01/220100130. I bet you've got a whole arsenal of deadly serious traps you've never dared to use, even against club members?``
``Heh heh heh heh heh heh!!@k@vS14/01/220100132. This is our chance to unleash everything on them!``
``Oh ho ho ho ho ho!!@k@vS14/04/220400047. You should be grateful that I'm part of this club!``

``I don't know who these people think they are, but if I'm free to go all out, they'll face the full array of miraculous traps I've held in reserve -- traps too devastating to use even on fellow club members!!``

``Ahaha, that's right!@k@vS14/02/220200044. Satoko-chan's traps have Mii-chan's personal seal of approval!@k@vS14/02/220200045. Even if we're up against an actual army, Satoko-chan's traps will scatter them all!!``
...They were heaping praise on Satoko, but of course, Rena had no intention of going easy on anyone either.
Rena usually seemed gentle and easygoing, but I knew what she was like when she got serious.
When it truly mattered, when a fight could not be avoided, she possessed a strength that yielded to no one -- that was Rena.
Once she unleashed that power... honestly, even I don't think I could beat her in a head-on confrontation...!

``...That's not true at all.@k@vS14/02/220200047. Keiichi-kun, your explosive power and snap judgment in critical moments are incredible.@k@vS14/02/220200048. I feel like you could shatter a fate that even I couldn't break through.``

``Heh heh heh!@k@vS14/06/220600168. If Kei-chan is a red-hot, blazing flame, then Rena-san is a blue flame that burns quiet and fierce.@k@vS14/06/220600169. Did you know? A blue flame actually burns hotter than a red one~``
...Oh, that's a great analogy! The man who burns red -- Keiichi Maebara!! The woman who burns blue -- Rena Ryuugu! What a perfect chance to show the world the caliber of our club!!
And on top of that, our club's unrivaled top duo holds down the critical positions! Each one the strongest alone, and together -- utterly invincible!! That's our club!!
``Well, I haven't been a club member for very long.@k@vS14/06/220600171. ...But Kei-chan -- no, Club Leader!@k@vS14/06/220600172. I promise I will never betray your expectations.``
``Let's teach those clueless fools a lesson about what happens when they cross the Sonozaki sisters without so much as a greeting!@k@vS14/06/220600174. Heh heh heh heh!!``

``...Heh heh heh heh.@k@vS14/03/220300083. You know... I was keeping quiet because I thought it'd be unseemly to say, but this old man's been thinking the same thing all along.@k@vS14/03/220300084. ...This is shaping up to be one hell of a good time!!``
``I'm grateful right now, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/03/220300086. Thanks for bringing such fine prey to our attention!!``
``Heh heh heh. The nerve, trying to pull something in our Hinamizawa without even paying our club a visit!@k@vS14/03/220300088. We'll make sure they learn proper etiquette!!``

Everyone was raring to go, flexing their arms in unison. Our morale had reached its peak. ...Despite facing a monstrous conspiracy, with all of us together, I didn't feel like we could possibly lose. ...This was the best feeling in the world!!
``Th-... thank you, everyone.@k@vS14/05/220500200. Thank you for believing me...``
Rika-chan's eyes glistened with emotion as she bowed deeply. ...At last, we had truly earned her trust. Knowing that only fueled my fighting spirit further.

``Right then -- time for a strategy meeting!@k@vS14/03/220300090. ...First, even if we hide Rika-chan at the Sonozaki house, we'll need a plausible reason for the move.@k@vS14/03/220300091. That's actually our biggest bottleneck...``
...A reason? Can't we just move her there and hide her...?

``If we suddenly move her, the enemy will get suspicious and wonder what happened.@k@vS14/03/220300093. The whole reason you've been operating in secret is to keep them off guard, right?@k@vS14/03/220300094. If we blow that, all our efforts go up in smoke.``
As expected, Mion was analyzing the situation with a cool head. For someone like me who tended toward brute force, having her as a strategist was invaluable.
``A reason, huh... hmm.@k@vS14/01/220100149. How about saying she fell ill after the festival and is bedridden at home?``
``Mii... That would fall apart quickly since nobody's actually in the house.@k@vS14/05/220500202. My home is under surveillance by the Mountain Dogs, you know.``
``Then I'll go back to the house.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1020. We could say that I'm there nursing Rika. How would that work?``

``That's no good either.@k@vS14/01/220100151. If they think Satoko knows where Rika is, she becomes a target.@k@vS14/01/220100152. Only the people in this room know that Satoko has woken up, so we shouldn't put her on their radar yet.``
``...Oh, what if the Coach helps us make it look like Rika-chan was admitted here?@k@vS14/01/220100154. Then we secretly move her to the Sonozaki house to hide... something like that?``

``Unfortunately, it's easy for them to verify whether someone is actually hospitalized here, and nearly impossible to fake.@k@vS14/10/22100260. And if they discover she's not here, the Sonozaki house would be the first place they'd suspect.@k@vS14/10/22100261. The risk is far too great.``

``Making someone appear to be somewhere they're not is the hard part...@k@vS14/06/220600176. Making someone who is present appear absent is much simpler, though. There are plenty of hiding spots for that.``
No doubt a barbed reference to Satoshi. ...The Coach winced and shrank apologetically when he caught the implication.

``Don't be too hard on him, Shion.@k@vS14/01/220100156. Now, regarding Takano-sa -- actually, there's no need for honorifics anymore.@k@vS14/01/220100157. Akasaka-san is currently investigating Takano to uncover the mastermind pulling the strings.``

``If he can get a grip on that person, the enemy's supply line --@k@vS14/01/220100159. in other words, their backing from headquarters -- gets cut off, and they won't be able to act.@k@vS14/01/220100160. Once that happens, we win.``
``But is that really enough?@k@vS14/02/220200050. Even if we identify the mastermind, Takano is still the one carrying out the plan, right?``
``Takano can only activate emergency measures under the manual once the mastermind authorizes the legitimacy of her actions.``
``So if we can publicly expose the mastermind's conspiracy, the emergency manual becomes nothing more than meaningless barbarism.``
``Given that Takano's goal is to validate her own theories, she would absolutely avoid that outcome.``
``But that's a relief.@k@vS14/02/220200052. Someone who remembered a promise from five whole years ago and came to help -- that's a truly wonderful person.``
``...Hau, I'm a little jealous...``
``...He's not my type, but Rika, I'll admit I'm a bit envious...``
``M-meep, it's not like that with Akasaka at all...``

``Plus, he's with the Public Security Bureau of the Tokyo Metropolitan Police, in a secret division no less!@k@vS14/01/220100162. That's an incredibly dependable ally!@k@vS14/01/220100163. A secret division -- how cool is that?!``

``However, Akasaka-san's role seems to be investigating the enemy's backbone, so we should think of Rika-chan's defense separately.@k@vS14/03/220300096. And then there's the more pressing matter of Tomitake-san, who's currently missing.``

``The fact that he's missing actually makes me think the odds of him being dead are low.@k@vS14/03/220300098. Because if he were dead, they would have presented his body as the curse's victim, not Teppei.``
``...Why was Teppei killed?``
``Most likely as a demonstration of Hinamizawa Syndrome for the mastermind's benefit.@k@vS14/03/220300100. A way of saying, 'Look at the symptoms -- people die from this.'``

``And for the villagers, making them believe it was the curse serves as a warning to keep them from fleeing Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/03/220300102. Besides, fear and anxiety are powerful tools for anyone looking to manipulate a population.``
``I imagine it also plays a role in controlling the villagers' collective behavior after Rika-chan is offered as a sacrifice.``

``Containment, in other words.@k@vS14/01/220100165. That's also why Takano's fake corpse was discovered so conspicuously -- it wasn't just about faking her death. It served a purpose like that, too.``
``Let's get back on topic.@k@vS14/03/220300105. ...If Tomitake-san is alive, whatever intelligence he may have gathered could very well decide the outcome of this entire battle.``

``...What's more, Tomitake-san seems to be the only one who can call in the Bloodhound unit capable of subduing the enemy.@k@vS14/03/220300107. In other words, Tomitake-san's fate could tip the scales of this entire conflict.``
``...Hmm!@k@vS14/03/220300109. To think that lousy photographer, old man Tomitake, is the critical figure who holds our fate in his hands -- what a strange turn of events~!``

``...That said, while I don't want to be overly pessimistic, there's a chance we won't be able to count on Tomitake-san's help.@k@vS14/06/220600178. Just to be safe, I think we should consider other options as well.``
``...But do we actually have any other cards to play?``
``Hmm~, the only other person we can count on is probably Ooishi.@k@vS14/03/220300111. The question is how to explain this to him~``
``On that note, I explained the situation to Ooishi when we came here last night.@k@vS14/05/220500208. Akasaka backed me up, but he still seemed unsure.``
That was only natural. Ooishi had been suddenly confronted with a truth that overturned everything he had long believed. He needed time to process it.
``...Also, Tomitake-san was conducting covert investigations from the inside, so he may have left some trace of his findings somewhere.``
``As insurance in case something happened to him.@k@vS14/03/220300114. We shouldn't get our hopes up, but it's worth looking into.``

``Exactly!@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1022. Any competent intelligence operative would have established an emergency contact protocol in case of unforeseen circumstances.@k@vS14/04/VT4Bc_satoko1023. Could there be clues left at Tomitake-san's lodgings?``

``But if the enemy suspected Tomitake-san,@k@vS14/02/220200056. ...his lodgings would be the very first place they'd search.@k@vS14/02/220200057. Tomitake-san must have known that, so I doubt he'd leave anything important there.``
``For now, let's think of alternatives that don't rely on Tomitake.@k@vS14/05/220500210. ...Irie, can you think of any other approach?``
We all turned our attention to the Coach, who had been sitting in silence this whole time. But he simply shook his head regretfully.
``I'm sorry... I left all communication with 'Tokyo' entirely to Tomitake-san and Takano-san, so I have no good means of reaching them...``

``Also, while it's true that the clinic staff are all out today, you should assume that most of them are loyal to Takano-san.@k@vS14/10/22100264. Shameful as it is to admit, there isn't much I can do to help you.``
He wore a pained expression after that honest confession, clearly uncomfortable.
Still, I was grateful to have even one ally from within the enemy's camp. And the hardest times for this man would come after everything was over, not now.
Now wasn't the time to ask the impossible of him.

``In that case... Akasaka-san's work really does become the linchpin.@k@vS14/03/220300116. Until then, all we can do is focus on ways to evade the enemy...@k@vS14/03/220300117. That's a pretty passive approach.``

``Besides, relying on Akasaka-san is reassuring but also risky.@k@vS14/01/220100167. We don't have a clear deadline for when he'll come through.@k@vS14/01/220100168. ...We need to think of another angle of attack.@k@vS14/01/220100169. Is there some kind of offensive move that could support Akasaka-san's investigation?``
``How exciting.@k@vS14/04/220400057. Set a trap, seize them by the tail, and shove undeniable proof right in the mastermind's face!@k@vS14/04/220400058. It's the ultimate trap scenario!!``
``...If their target is me...@k@vS14/05/220500212. then what if I served as bait?@k@vS14/05/220500213. It's dangerous, but since I'm their objective, they'll have no choice but to take the bait.``
``That's a very risky move.@k@vS14/02/220200059. ...But it's true -- Takano's pursuit of Rika-chan could give us a chance to secure major evidence.``

``If she deliberately shows herself, escalates the commotion, and turns it into a chase... hmm.@k@vS14/03/220300119. We could expect some rather interesting results from that!@k@vS14/03/220300120. Heh heh heh -- and that's where the mountains behind Satoko's house come into play!``
``Well, if we fled into those mountains, our pursuers would be in for quite the ordeal~!``
...Satoko's wicked grin was one thing we could count on right now. This was going to be spectacular!! Things were really heating up!!

``Still, I think that should be our very last resort.@k@vS14/02/220200061. If we retreat to the mountains, it becomes a siege. The whole situation changes drastically.@k@vS14/02/220200062. We should only play that card when we're truly out of options.``

``That's true.@k@vS14/04/220400061. The mountain traps would be extraordinarily effective as a defense, but defense is all they are.@k@vS14/04/220400062. They're entirely different from an actual offensive against the enemy.``

``Right. The mountain siege would be our last trump card when we're cornered.@k@vS14/03/220300122. A true last resort.@k@vS14/03/220300123. Only to be used if the enemy attacks my house and we're forced to abandon it as a base.``
``By the way, there's a hidden tunnel in our basement that leads out to a well in the mountains.``

That's incredible...! This keeps getting better and better!!! A mysterious enemy organization, covert operations, a secret base, and a hidden underground passage!! If this doesn't get your blood pumping, you're not alive!!

``Heh heh heh!@k@vS14/06/220600180. You don't need to be a man for this to get your blood pumping!@k@vS14/06/220600181. Anyway, our general direction is taking shape.@k@vS14/06/220600182. While we're at it, does anyone have a more concrete and effective plan?``
I gave a firm nod to Shion's question. We needed one more solid idea to push us over the top.

All our plans so far had been essentially defensive. What we needed was something more aggressive -- something perfectly suited to our club. A devastating blow that would bring the enemy's conspiracy crashing down...!!
``...Do we really have the means for something like that...?``

``Yes. I'm sure we do.@k@vS14/02/220200064. We're not as powerless as you think, Rika-chan. And we're not without our own trump card.@k@vS14/02/220200065. ...We have you -- the key person they need -- right here with us.``
``That itself is a critical advantage.@k@vS14/02/220200067. And I don't just mean using Rika-chan as bait.``
``Exactly.@k@vS14/03/220300126. The simple fact that the queen carrier is in our hands gives us a significant edge over them.``

``...What is their objective?@k@vS14/03/220300128. Kill Rika-chan, activate the so-called emergency manual, annihilate this entire village, and use it all for a factional power struggle within their organization!@k@vS14/03/220300129. Correct?``
``That's right.@k@vS14/06/220600184. To them, Rika-chama is like a bomb that could blow everything sky-high.``
``...Their plan is to detonate that bomb, create massive chaos, and exploit the fallout for their own gain.``
``And our victory condition is preventing that detonation.@k@vS14/04/220400065. ...Which isn't exactly an enviable position to be in.``

``Indeed.@k@vS14/03/220300131. We have to maintain our defenses indefinitely, while the enemy wins the moment they manage to kill Rika-chan.@k@vS14/03/220300132. ...It's like playing a game of soccer where only one side has a goal.``

``What's more, until we can read their attack patterns and intentions, even if we repel one assault, there could be another.@k@vS14/02/220200069. Defense alone might be manageable, but facing a second and third wave would wear us thin.``
Just as Mion crossed her arms in thought -- that was when it happened.
``......Shion? What is it?``
``Oh, it's nothing.@k@vS14/06/220600189. Everyone, listen.``
``...I have one good plan.``
Shion, who had been lost in thought with her head bowed, looked up and spoke.
``Coach.@k@vS14/06/220600192. Call the police right now.@k@vS14/06/220600193. ...Tell them an armed criminal with a gun has broken into the clinic.``
``Sh-Shion?!@k@vS14/03/220300135. W-wait, you mean--``
Mion blurted out in surprise, but Shion remained perfectly composed.
``I'm sorry, Rika-chama.@k@vS14/06/220600195. ...Would you be willing to die?``
About ten minutes after the emergency call from the Irie Clinic, two patrol cars screeched to a halt at the entrance, and officers poured inside. Ooishi-san followed close behind them.

Ooishi-san's face contorted in shock, his brows knitting tightly as he stood frozen. On the waiting room floor, surrounded by police officers, Mion and I had Shion pinned down as she thrashed wildly, her eyes bloodshot with murderous fury.
``--Damn it!!@k@vS14/06/220600197. Let go, let go of meee!!@k@vS14/06/220600198. I'll kill you! I'll kill every last one of youuu!!``
``Ngh-- Hey!!@k@vS14/01/220100184. What the hell has gotten into you?!``
``Come on-- that's enough, Shion!!@k@vS14/03/220300137. How far does your paranoia about the Sonozakis have to go before you're satisfied?!``
Mion and I both had cuts on our arms and faces as we struggled desperately to hold Shion down.
Several officers watching us rushed over to help restrain her, which only made Shion fight harder to break free.
But she was outnumbered and outmuscled. Overpowered by the adults' strength, Shion's expression went slack as she was finally forced flat against the floor.
``...Sonozaki Shion, you are under arrest for assault and obstruction of justice!``
``Let me go, damn youuu!!@k@vS14/06/220600200. If I'm a criminal, then so are all of them!!@k@vS14/06/220600201. The director of this place took Satoshi-kun... Satoshi-kuuuun!!``
``They're the ones who should rot in hell!!@k@vS14/06/220600203. Waaaaaahhhh!!!``
Even as the handcuffs snapped onto her wrists, Shion continued screaming and hurling curses at the Coach, who stood ashen-faced in the back of the room.
Ooishi-san watched the scene in a daze, then snapped back to himself. He came over to where I sat slumped on the floor and grasped my shoulder, peering into my face.
``M-Maebara-san...!@k@vS14/11/220700209. What in the world happened here?!``
``...It's exactly what it looks like.@k@vS14/01/220100186. When we came to visit Satoko, Shion was trying to shoot the Coach with a modified model gun...``
``She was screaming, 'I'll send you to hell too for what you did to Satoshi-kun'...@k@vS14/03/220300139. I have no idea what she's even talking about...!``
Mion's voice caught in her throat as she said that, and she buried her face in my chest, sobbing.
Ooishi-san stared blankly at the scene, but then looked around and realized with a start that someone who should have been there was conspicuously absent.
``S-Sonozaki-san!@k@vS14/11/220700211. Just now, you said that Shion Sonozaki said 'you too'?!@k@vS14/11/220700212. D-don't tell me there was someone else?!``
``...Yes.@k@vS14/03/220300141. She said, 'I've taken Rika-chama. If you won't talk, I'll make her tell me instead.'``
``G-good lord...!@k@vS14/11/220700214. K-Kuma-chan!!@k@vS14/11/220700215. Check the Furude residence!@k@vS14/11/220700216. And launch a search for Furude Rika -- gather any eyewitness information!!``
``R-roger!!``
At Ooishi-san's command, the detective named Kumagai led several officers charging outside. Before long, a siren wailed and a car peeled away with urgency.
Forensics personnel were swarming the clinic conducting their investigation. Ignoring all of it, Ooishi sank to his knees on the floor and muttered bitterly.

``Ah, haha...@k@vS14/11/220700218. Actually, when I stepped outside for a smoke last night and came back in, I thought I sensed someone out there.@k@vS14/11/220700219. They moved so quickly I assumed it was just a cat, but...``
``To think it was actually Sonozaki Shion... Ngh...!@k@vS14/11/220700221. Even if my mind was in turmoil, making a blunder like that means I'm not fit to call myself a detective...``
``Ha, haha... I suppose my decision came too late...@k@vS14/11/220700223. Does this mean I've lost my chance to respond to what we discussed yesterday?``
``They say hindsight is twenty-twenty, but...@k@vS14/11/220700225. ...have I truly lost my chance to unravel the mystery of the curse forever...?``
``............It's not over yet.``
Ooishi looked up sharply when he heard Mion's voice -- soft as a whisper, but razor-sharp and full of conviction.
We casually glanced around, then leaned close to him under the pretense of breaking down in tears, and whispered what needed to be said.
``(......You kids are... what...?!)``

``(...After this, 'interrogate' Shion and confirm the details.@k@vS14/01/220100191. From there, I leave everything to your judgment, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/01/220100192. If you think it's too dangerous, just forget this conversation ever happened.)``
``(So you've... made your move...?)@k@vS14/11/220700228. Understood.@k@vS14/11/220700229. I won't pry any further at this point. I'll come by again later for a follow-up.``
Ooishi stood up as if nothing had happened and headed for the exit. On his way out, he gave the faintest of nods before disappearing through the door.
``Don't worry.@k@vS14/01/220100194. ...But still.``
``What?``
``You... are really good at fake crying.``
Beside me, Shion sat with her head hanging low, a jacket draped over her. Her wrists were handcuffed, and her meek composure made her earlier rampage seem like something from a different world.
Still... these kids really had gone this far... Even among trusting friends, to stake everything on such a reckless gamble...

``...I'll have you know, modified or not, that model gun still falls under the Firearms and Swords Control Act.@k@vS14/11/220700231. And since you were caught red-handed for assault, you could face actual prison time even under juvenile law if things go badly.``
Shion stayed silent, head still bowed. ...No, wait -- I could make out the faint sound of her breathing, and when I turned to look... was that a smile playing at her lips?
So she'd factored that in too. Impressive. Truly worthy of being a Sonozaki.
``Well then, we'll take our time with the interrogation.@k@vS14/11/220700234. ...Let me hear what you have to say.``
Night was beginning to fall.
It was that threshold hour when the day shift and night shift traded places.
...Okonogi Gardeners, under cover of that transition, shed its daytime facade and transformed into a forward operating base.
That said, since nothing had happened so far, the atmosphere was relaxed enough that some men were playing hanafuda at the reception table.
Being a base, dozing off was naturally prohibited, but there was no need to maintain constant tension.
As a result, personnel not assigned to specific tasks often killed time watching television or playing hanafuda and card games.
Then, abruptly, the phone rang.
They had placed a phone near the reception area so it could be answered immediately.
``...Thank you for calling, this is Okonogi Gardeners.@k@vS14/14/223400002. ......Yes.@k@vS14/14/223400003. ......What?!``
The man's expression changed instantly, his posture snapping upright. His knee banged against the desk as he stood, but he paid it no mind...
Takano burst into the underground security room, her hair in disarray.
``Apologies for the urgent summons, Major!!``
``Status report!@k@vS14/09/220900005. Is this confirmed?!``
``Yes, ma'am.@k@vS14/14/223400006. At 6:15 PM, Sonozaki Shion reportedly began making suggestive statements during interrogation at the Okinomiya station.``
``That's impossible!@k@vS14/09/220900007. What about R's surveillance?!``
``About that, Major...@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1005. ...there is something we haven't reported to you yet, as the information was unverified.``

``Last night... R apparently fell into the same swamp where Tomitake went under, while she was wandering through the mountains.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1007. Several team members witnessed a village boy desperately searching for her...``
``Wh-why wasn't something this critical reported to me immediately?!@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1002. If anything happens to R before we execute the plan, it's all over!``
``Whether the information is confirmed or unconfirmed, report it to me first before making that judgment!@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1004. ...Wait, hold on.``
``Then... what exactly does it mean that Sonozaki Shion killed R?!``

``Well... the witnesses were apparently so shaken that their account of the situation was incomplete... so this is merely a conjecture, but...@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1009. there's a possibility that Sonozaki Shion herself lured R to the swamp and pushed her in.``
``And that's precisely why the Sonozaki family, backing her, would be concealing information about R's fate... keeping the truth hidden from the villagers as well.``
``If that's the case... the claim that R was murdered carries considerable credibility.``
Takano felt as though every drop of blood in her body had frozen solid.
Furude Rika's assassination was already a settled matter.
The body was to be discovered at the Furude Shrine the day after tomorrow, after which everything would proceed according to the emergency manual.
But all of that hinged on Rika's death as the starting point. Without confirmed proof of her death, pushing forward with the plan would render the entire thing meaningless.
Yet at this rate, not only would the emergency manual be impossible to activate -- even the 'Ritual' that Tokyo headquarters desired would be difficult... no, impossible to carry out.
If only... if only they could just find the body...!!
``Search for R immediately!!@k@vS14/09/220900010. Report every last detail of Sonozaki Shion's testimony to me!!@k@vS14/09/220900011. Secure the body before the police can!!``
``Above all, scour the swamp where R supposedly fell!!@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1007. Redirect the team searching for Tomitake Jirou to that location!!``
``We're already searching the swamp with diving equipment as we speak.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1013. ...But the swamp is deep and incredibly murky, so it's going to take considerable time.``

``No personal effects have been found, and there are no signs of anyone having climbed out of the swamp...@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1015. And none of the villagers seem to have heard anything about R going missing.``
She had blundered. ...Takano bit down on her lip, filled with bitter regret at her own overreach.
Her original plan had been to manipulate Shion into suspecting the Sonozaki family, thereby sowing confusion among the police and the people around her.
The more Shion stirred things up, the more the police and villagers' attention would be diverted from their own activities. Shion was nothing more than a puppet -- a useful fool.

...But she had never anticipated that the fallout would reach Furude Rika. Had she underestimated Sonozaki Shion's capacity for action...? No -- it was precisely that capacity that had made Shion seem like an ideal decoy, and that decision had now completely backfired...!
``Th-then why hasn't anyone raised an alarm about R's disappearance?!``
``Even an ordinary child going missing would cause a commotion -- let alone the greatest symbol of the village! Word should have spread across the entire village by now!!``
``The villagers all seem to believe R is resting at home.``

``In fact, multiple witnesses saw her collapse after her ritual dance, complaining of feeling ill, and being examined by Director Irie.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1018. Afterward, one of the adults apparently carried her home, where she fell asleep.``
``Th-then, is R at home?@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1011. Or isn't she?!``

``No, we dispatched someone to check immediately.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1020. ...It appears she isn't there.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1021. The villagers most likely don't know that R left her home and went into the mountains.``
``Th-then why did R go into the mountains in the first place?!``
``That much, I don't know.@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1023. ...However.``
``However... what?!``
``Major... if you'll permit me a hypothesis...@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1025. R may have already been killed at the hands of Sonozaki Shion.``
``Based on our team's reports, I believe we can proceed under that assumption without issue.``
``Already dead... you say?``
``You're all going to conclude she was murdered based on circumstantial evidence alone, without even finding a body?!``
``Don't be absurd. There is no way we can activate the emergency manual under such uncertain conditions!``
``Without confirmed proof of Furude Rika's death, our village-wide containment operation loses all legitimacy and becomes nothing more than indiscriminate slaughter!!``
Takano's agitation wasn't simply because she had been jolted from sleep.
...No. What she felt was fear.

Losing the queen carrier didn't merely mean a delay in their plans. If the subject's death were confirmed more than 48 hours after the fact, the central premise of the Hinamizawa Syndrome research that she and her grandfather had devoted their lives to would collapse entirely.
If that happened, the faction in Tokyo that had entrusted her with funding and authority would raise an uproar, and every last one of her plans would dissolve like foam on water...
And yet -- what would happen if they forced the operation through regardless?
Nothing of value. It would be nothing more than a massive incident, contributing absolutely nothing to protecting the thesis her grandfather had believed in all his life.
In short, without Furude Rika's sacrifice, the 'Ritual' could not begin, and everything her grandfather had researched would simply be buried in darkness.
``Excuse me, Major!@k@vS14/00/hiba0111002. You have a phone call from Nomura-sama in Tokyo!``
``...Tch...@k@vS14/09/220900022. ...How does that woman always catch wind of things so quickly...!``

``While I'm on the phone, intensify the interrogation of Sonozaki Shion at the Okinomiya station -- immediately!!@k@vS14/09/220900024. I don't care what methods you use -- find out whether R is alive and where she is, now!!``
``Already underway!@k@vS14/14/223400018. We've been informed that prefectural headquarters is deploying personnel to break her, no matter what it takes!!``
changeReason: New translation adds location detail not in original: 'prefectural headquarters'.

TextENGNew: ``On top of that, all available personnel are searching the entire village to locate her...!!``
``Not 'searching'!@k@vS14/09/220900026. Find her!!@k@vS14/09/220900027. This is top priority!!@k@vS14/09/220900028. Make them hurry!!!``
``...Hee hee. Good morning. This is Takano.``
``......Huh...?@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1019. W-wait, please! At this point, you can't be serious...?!``
``Ngh...!@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1021. N-no... that's not what's happening...!@k@vS14/09/VT4Bc_takano1022. P-please, just a little more time...!``
All unit members had been assembled on emergency alert at Okonogi Gardeners, the Mountain Dogs' garrison in Okinomiya.

``Right, understood.@k@vS14/14/223400021. The moment you learn anything from the Okinomiya station, report to both me and the Major!@k@vS14/14/223400022. More importantly! Why was R's status not verified?!``
changeReason: New translation adds location detail not in original: 'okinomiya station'.

TextENGNew: ``S-several team members heard a loud splash -- distinctly different from the one when Tomitake Jirou fell in.``
``We've also confirmed that the boy calling out her name by the swamp is one of R's friends. There's virtually no doubt.``
``...Then why didn't that kid alert the other villagers?@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1028. Are we absolutely certain he wasn't the one deceiving us?``
``No chance!@k@vS14/49/VT4Bc_13hibari1011. Our concealment was flawless. There's no way that boy could have detected our presence!``

True enough -- Okonogi himself had floated the idea that it might be a deception... but he simply couldn't conceive of any motive for a boy named Maebara Keiichi to conspire with Sonozaki Shion to kill R.
And yet... something nagged at him. ...What if R had sensed their covert operations and divulged the information to that boy?
It was unlikely, but if it were true, then they were the ones being played for fools...
``...Commander. If you still have doubts, shall we apprehend the boy immediately?@k@vS14/49/VT4Bc_13hibari1013. Under the current circumstances, abducting him would be simple...``
``We can't do that without the Major's authorization!@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1030. Besides, if we kidnap him, the police will launch an investigation, and that only makes things more complicated.``
``In any case, as a precaution... monitor that kid's location and movements!@k@vS14/14/VT4Bc_okono1032. And pour everything we have into searching the village!!``
``Commander... is there any chance R has been taken outside the village?@k@vS14/00/hiba0111006. Other than the Sonozaki residence, I can't think of any suitable location.``

``...Don't let your thinking calcify!@k@vS14/14/223400030. Consider other possibilities!!@k@vS14/14/223400031. ...In the first place, how could one girl spirit R out of the village in broad daylight without being seen by anyone?!``
Okonogi's instincts -- long rusted but still sharp -- sounded an alarm.

...What if Sonozaki Shion's claim of kidnapping Furude Rika served some entirely different purpose? ...It was deeply unsettling to realize that they had intended to seize the initiative, only to find themselves outmaneuvered instead.
``...Come to think of it, that Public Security agent from the Metropolitan Police has been sniffing around.@k@vS14/14/223400033. Has his location in Okinomiya been confirmed?``
``...Most likely.@k@vS14/00/ugu0111004. I'll have it verified.``
``Also, what's the status on First Lieutenant Tomitake's whereabouts?@k@vS14/14/223400035. We still haven't found him?!``
``N-not yet...@k@vS14/00/hiba0111008. We've combed the entire area around the swamp, but there's no sign of anything surfacing or anything having sunk.``
``Ngh...!!@k@vS14/14/223400037. No, wait.@k@vS14/14/223400038. ...The Major just received a phone call, didn't she?@k@vS14/14/223400039. From Tokyo--``
``What about it...?``
``...Public Security from the Metropolitan Police is sniffing around out here... which means...``
``Damn it -- we've been outplayed!!``
``...Well now, there's no turning back.``
``Heh heh heh. Now let's see what comes tumbling out when we shake the mountains...``
Kumagai trembled with eager anticipation.
``How is Sonozaki Shion doing?``

``She's calm.@k@vS14/19/223100023. ...More like completely relaxed, actually.@k@vS14/19/223100024. She wasn't kidding about having been thrown in detention before -- she's snoring away without a care in the world.``
``Ha ha ha!@k@vS14/11/220700237. What else would you expect from the demon girl? She must get that audacious nerve from her mother.``
He was both astonished and, at the same time, genuinely impressed.
No matter how experienced you are, nobody wants to be in police custody. And yet, she had willingly sacrificed herself just to ensure her friends' safety...
``Kuma-chan.@k@vS14/11/220700239. I'm going to check on the communications center.@k@vS14/11/220700240. Guard this place with your life.``
``Yes sir!!@k@vS14/19/223100026. Nobody's getting through here, over my dead body!!``

``...My next task is Houjou Teppei's judicial autopsy.@k@vS14/00/223000027. I've packed the team with people I've personally trained, so not a single micron of tampering will slip past us!!@k@vS14/00/223000028. Kumagai, I'm counting on you to hold things down here!``
``You can count on me!!!``

Kumagai flashed a defiant grin and a thumbs up, then stepped into the interrogation room and closed the door. Forty-eight hours from the time of arrest. ...Whether by coincidence or design, that was the time limit fate had given them for this battle.
Then, Ooishi made his way to the communications center floor.

All radio transmissions and phone calls were routed through there first. During daytime hours, the front desk would also take calls, but after hours, everything went through the communications center.
``How are things here?``
``Well...@k@vS14/00/centA11002. just a few minutes ago, there was an outside call that came in on the direct line to the Criminal Investigation Division.``
``From whom?``
``Chief Inspector Ootaka, Prefectural Police Headquarters.``
changeReason: New translation adds location detail not in original: 'prefectural police'.

TextENGNew: Ooishi allowed himself a grin.
So the enemy had begun to move. They had supposedly planted a spy within the police force through bribery. ...And that spy had taken the bait, hook, line, and sinker.
But to think it was actually Ootaka, of all people.
Ootaka was an insufferably smug intellectual, diametrically opposed to Ooishi's field-first philosophy. Even back when Ooishi was at prefectural headquarters, they had been at each other's throats.
changeReason: Different locations referenced: old mentions 'prefectural police', new mentions 'prefectural headquarters'.

TextENGNew: ``The call has ended.``
``...Let me check with Kuma-chan.@k@vS14/11/220700246. 'Beep, boop, bop!' And... hello?@k@vS14/11/220700247. Whoa -- easy, it's just me.@k@vS14/11/220700248. So, how did it go?``

``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/19/223100029. Every last one of his responses was straight out of the playbook!@k@vS14/19/223100030. That guy is absolutely livid.@k@vS14/19/223100031. He says he'll be barging in here first thing in the morning, as soon as the station chief arrives.``
``Same as always -- he loves making a spectacle of things.@k@vS14/11/220700250. Can't even fight his own battles, that one.@k@vS14/11/220700251. Nfu fu fu!``
``Things are really heating up now!@k@vS14/19/223100033. I could smell something rotten in every word he said!!@k@vS14/19/223100034. I can't wait to see their next move~!!``
Well now, things were getting interesting indeed. What would come next? Come now, demons -- step right this way. Come in closer!

``...Hmm...@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1040. That said, if that idiot Ootaka is coming... I'd better take extra precautions, just to be safe.@k@vS14/11/VT4Bc_oishi1041. Oh my, look at the hour. I hope he's still around...``
Muttering to himself, he checked the wall clock and reached for the receiver... when, just then.
``...Oh, that reminds me, Ooishi-san.@k@vS14/00/centA11006. The Hinamizawa town council dropped off a bag -- apparently it was lost property from the day of the festival.``
``A bag...?@k@vS14/11/220700253. What kind? What's in it?``
``I took a look earlier.@k@vS14/00/centA11008. Appears to be a camera and some rolls of film...@k@vS14/00/centA11009. What should we do with it?``
